responses.question_text: What is the main reason you want to start your own business?
responses.response_value: solve-problems

responses.question_text: When you imagine your business being successful, what does that look like?
responses.response_value: A small but profitable SaaS business with 5-10 employees, where I have full ownership and work on problems I care about. Revenue around $500K, working with a tight team.

responses.question_text: How comfortable are you with taking risks?
Rate your current satisfaction in different life areas
Rank these values in order of importance to you
responses.response_value: [{"value":"personal-freedom","label":"Personal Freedom"},{"value":"learning","label":"Learning"},{"value":"making-difference","label":"Making a Difference"},{"value":"financial-success","label":"Financial Success"},{"value":"family-time","label":"Family Time"},{"value":"security","label":"Security"},{"value":"adventure","label":"Adventure"},{"value":"recognition","label":"Recognition"}]

responses.question_text: Describe your ideal life 10 years from now
responses.response_value: Running a bootstrapped software company from anywhere in Europe. Financially free, working 40 hours a week on products I built, with time for family and hobbies.

responses.question_text: How confident are you that you can achieve your 10-year vision?
How clear is your business idea right now?
responses.response_value: somewhat-clear

responses.question_text: Describe your current business idea
responses.response_value: A B2B SaaS platform that helps small e-commerce businesses automate their customer support with AI, reducing response times from hours to seconds.

responses.question_text: How passionate are you about this business idea/area?
How well do you understand the problem your business would solve?
Rate your current skill level in areas relevant to your business idea
What are the biggest skill gaps you need to address?
responses.response_value: Sales and B2B marketing are my biggest gaps. I can build the product but need to learn how to acquire customers and structure SaaS pricing.

responses.question_text: Who is your target customer?
responses.response_value: Small to medium e-commerce stores (Shopify, WooCommerce) with 5-50 employees, doing $500K-$5M in annual revenue.

responses.question_text: What specific problems do your target customers face?
responses.response_value: Overwhelmed by repetitive customer questions (shipping, returns, tracking). Can not afford full-time support staff. Losing sales due to slow response times.

responses.question_text: How do customers currently solve these problems?
responses.response_value: Generic live chat tools (Intercom, Zendesk) that are too expensive for small stores. DIY FAQ pages. Hiring part-time support staff.

responses.question_text: What makes your solution different or better?
responses.response_value: AI trained specifically on e-commerce, connects to order management for real-time tracking, 10x cheaper than Zendesk for SMBs.

responses.question_text: How large is your target market?
responses.response_value: large

responses.question_text: Is your target market growing, stable, or declining?
responses.response_value: rapid-growth

responses.question_text: What revenue potential do you see for your business?
responses.response_value: growth-business

responses.question_text: How would you rate the timing for this business opportunity?
How competitive is this market?
responses.response_value: moderate

responses.question_text: What are the main barriers to entering this market?
responses.response_value: Technical complexity of AI, integrations with multiple e-commerce platforms, building trust with skeptical merchants.

responses.question_text: How would you rate the probability of success for this business idea?
Have you conducted interviews with potential customers?
responses.response_value: few

responses.question_text: What did you learn from customer conversations?
responses.response_value: Spoke with 3 Shopify store owners. All confirmed the problem - they spend 2-3 hours daily on repetitive support. Two said they would pay $50-100/month for automation.

responses.question_text: List your main competitors
responses.response_value: Direct: Tidio, Gorgias, Re:amaze. Indirect: Zendesk, Intercom (too expensive/generic for SMBs).

responses.question_text: What is your sustainable competitive advantage?
responses.response_value: Deep order management integration, AI fine-tuned on e-commerce conversations, pricing targeting $30-80/month that larger competitors ignore.

responses.question_text: How will your business make money?
responses.response_value: subscription

responses.question_text: What is your pricing strategy?
responses.response_value: Starter: $29/month (500 AI conversations), Growth: $79/month (2000 conversations), Pro: $199/month (unlimited). Free 14-day trial, no credit card required.

responses.question_text: What are the key activities your business must perform?
responses.response_value: Product development, AI model training, customer onboarding, content marketing for SEO, partnership with Shopify app ecosystem.

responses.question_text: What key resources does your business need?
responses.response_value: Engineering team (2-3 devs), AI/ML infrastructure, Shopify Partner status, $50K initial runway for 12 months.

responses.question_text: How will you validate your product concept?
responses.response_value: Build MVP with Shopify integration and basic AI. Recruit 5 beta stores for free 30-day trial. Track resolution rate, satisfaction scores, and hours saved.

responses.question_text: Who are your test users?
responses.response_value: 5 Shopify fashion/apparel stores ($200K-$1M revenue), recruited through LinkedIn and Shopify Facebook groups, currently using email-only support.

responses.question_text: What feedback mechanisms will you use?
responses.response_value: Weekly 30-min calls, in-app NPS surveys, dashboard showing resolution rate vs human escalation rate, session recordings for UX feedback.

responses.question_text: What are your key growth strategies?
responses.response_value: 1. Shopify App Store listing as primary distribution 2. SEO content targeting Shopify customer support keywords 3. Partnership with Shopify Plus agencies 4. Customer referral program.

responses.question_text: How will you allocate resources?
responses.response_value: 60% engineering, 25% sales/marketing, 15% customer success. Bootstrap first year, Series A after $500K ARR.

responses.question_text: What partnerships are essential?
responses.response_value: Shopify Partner Program, WooCommerce ecosystem, 2-3 boutique e-commerce agencies, OpenAI API partnership.

responses.question_text: How will you test your business model in the market?
responses.response_value: Shopify App Store launch with freemium. Target 100 installs in month 1, 20% free-to-paid conversion. Monitor churn, expansion revenue, CAC by channel.

responses.question_text: What metrics will you track?
responses.response_value: MRR growth, churn rate (<5% monthly), CAC by channel, LTV:CAC (3:1 target), AI resolution rate (>70% target), NPS (>50 target).

responses.question_text: What is your timeline for prototype validation?
responses.response_value: M1-2: 5 beta customers, validate AI rate. M3-4: App launch, 100 installs. M5-6: $5K MRR. M7-12: Scale to $25K MRR.
